description: Lecture 17 discusses No-Regret Dynamics in Algorithmic Game Theory, focusing on how players can reach equilibria quickly and the implications of regret minimization in single-player contexts.
topic_summary: The lecture covers the concept of No-Regret Dynamics, contrasting it with Best Response Dynamics, and explores its applications in various game settings. It emphasizes the importance of understanding regret minimization in both single-player and multiplayer scenarios, and how it relates to achieving approximate equilibria [t=61:89].
